name: man
description: Format and display the on-line manual pages

options.description: Specify the configuration file to use
Specify the list of directories to search (colon separated)
Specify the pager program
Specify which browser to use for HTML files
Specify a command that renders HTML files as text
Specify a colon-separated list of manual sections to search
Open every matching page instead of just the first
Reformat the source page, even when an up-to-date cat-page exists
Don't actually display the pages (dry run)
Both display and print debugging info
Equivalent to `whatis`
Format only, do not display
Print a help message and exit
Equivalent to apropos
Search for a given string in all pages
Specify an alternate set of pages to search based on the system name given
Specify the sequence of preprocessors to run before nroff or troff
Use `/usr/bin/groff -Tps -mandoc -c` to format the page
Print the location of files that would be displayed
Print file locations, one per line
